speaker: narrator
text: The first bell tolled like a struck rib: copper and cold. Millbrook answered.

speaker: narrator
text: Buckets thumped into hands. Barrels rolled to the well. Children were shooed under cellar hatches that smelled of earth, turnips, and fear. On the lane, chalk marks glowed faint in lantern light -- Serana's secular ward-lines. Here hold. Here yield.

speaker: narrator
text: Fall back to square at second bell. The guard captain's voice ran along the wall:

speaker: unknown
text: "Steady on -- by files -- mind your spacing."

speaker: narrator
text: They listened because of the charter-sigil on Serana's cloak and because Vale said

speaker: unknown
text: "Do it,"

speaker: narrator
text: and Vale had been telling them what to do for twelve winters and they were still alive.

speaker: narrator
text: Wind carried the wrong smell: damp stone, old iron, something like cold breath blown through a keyhole. Then the first of the shadow-thralls stepped out from behind the butcher's, bending wrong at the knee.

speaker: serana
text: "Hold until I call,"

speaker: narrator
text: Serana said, shield lifting, voice level. Her light flickered -- she made it steady with will alone.

speaker: narrator
text: Vale moved along the line like a man checking knots before a storm.

speaker: serana
text: "Spears up. Eyes forward. When the paladin calls, you move. Not before."

speaker: narrator
text: He stopped at a boy of perhaps sixteen -- spear too long, knuckles white on the haft. Vale squared the angle with one hand, said nothing. Then, quieter than the line needed to hear:

speaker: serana
text: "You showed up. That's the part most men don't manage."

speaker: narrator
text: He moved on.

speaker: narrator
text: The party split to assignments with the efficiency of people who'd learned to trust each other's competence even when they didn't trust each other.

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik took the wall, tool-harness mostly empty but hands steady on his axes. He touched the loops where charges used to nest -- six left, maybe seven. He'd counted three times.

speaker: serana
text: "Make them count,"

speaker: narrator
text: he muttered to no one.

speaker: narrator
text: Vex ghosted to the tower stairs, testing her wrapped shoulder with a roll that pulled but held. Another week and she'd forget it had been cut. For now, she'd just have to be faster.

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin held the center near the square, right arm bound tight under his sleeve. The scale pulsed coal-glow -- manageable, steady. He flexed his fingers. Pain sang through the lattice but didn't scream. He could work with that.

speaker: narrator
text: Nyxara took position at the gate, gloves immaculate, face calm. Her fingers had stiffened through the day but still flexed when she needed them to. Still worked. That would have to be enough.

speaker: narrator
text: Durgan moved to the bucket-line's edge, blades loose in their sheaths, shadow obedient at his boots in the lantern-glow. For now.

speaker: narrator
text: The thralls came on too quiet.

speaker: narrator
text: Vex met the first two in a blur -- dagger under ribs, second blade across the strange tendons behind a knee. The silhouettes buckled as anatomy demanded; their shadows whipped, failing to find purchase against Serana's ward-line.

speaker: serana
text: "On me!"

speaker: narrator
text: Serana barked, stepping to take a rake meant for a baker at the bucket line. Something stirred at her shoulder -- habit, reflex -- guttered before it could hold. She put her body between the blow and the question and did not flinch.

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik thumbed a charge and skipped it off the cobbles; it caromed under three thralls and went off with a shuddering cough of light and sound. One collapsed. Two more staggered, smoking.

speaker: serana
text: "Five left,"

speaker: narrator
text: he grunted, already counting.

speaker: narrator
text: Durgan's blades worked short and economical, every cut a lesson. His shadow dragged at him slightly, wanting, but he crushed it flat with jaw-locked effort and felt the warm trickle of a nosebleed vanish into the black of his collar. No one saw.

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin kept center, throwing small heat -- lines on steel, a melted hinge, a slick made glassy at the right instant. Pain flared, then steadied. Manageable.

speaker: narrator
text: A scream cut through the chaos -- civilian down in the square, leg twisted wrong.

speaker: narrator
text: A voice cut through the noise -- not a shout, a command, the kind that had been obeyed on fields worse than this:

speaker: serana
text: "Don't move him! Spine's compromised!"

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin turned.

speaker: narrator
text: A man knelt over the fallen villager -- grey-bearded, built like two decades of campaign work that had never quite softened, hands moving with the unhurried speed of someone who'd stopped being surprised by injury somewhere around middle age.

speaker: narrator
text: Battered medical bag, open on bloody cobbles. He didn't look up.

speaker: serana
text: "You."

speaker: narrator
text: This at a woman nearby.

speaker: serana
text: "Neck brace. My bag. Green strap."

speaker: narrator
text: Then to the wounded man, in the gentler voice of a carpenter addressing a split beam:

speaker: serana
text: "You're going to live. I've pulled men through worse than this with less. The bad news is you're going to remember it."

speaker: narrator
text: Then to Caelin, still not looking up:

speaker: serana
text: "Are you bleeding?"

speaker: serana
text: "No."

speaker: serana
text: "Then step aside. I have a limited amount of time and an unlimited amount of injured people."

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin blinked, then grabbed a dropped spear and handed it to a shaking boy who needed something to hold more than he needed direction. When he looked back, the man had already moved to the next patient.

speaker: narrator
text: Later, during a lull, he watched him splint a broken leg with a spear-shaft and someone's belt.

speaker: serana
text: "Good news,"

speaker: narrator
text: the man told the screaming patient.

speaker: serana
text: "You'll live. You'll walk with a limp that'll get you out of digging latrine pits for the rest of your natural life. In my experience that's a net positive."

speaker: narrator
text: The thralls retreated like smoke pulled backward through cracks.

speaker: narrator
text: Second bell rang.

speaker: serana
text: "Fall back to the square!"

speaker: narrator
text: Vale's shout carried like a thrown rope.

speaker: serana
text: "Mill Lane! Paladin -- anchor!"

speaker: serana
text: "We're there,"

speaker: narrator
text: Serana answered, already moving.

speaker: narrator
text: The square was a box of breath and noise -- barrows shoved into a wall, barrels upright for the bucket chain, a slapped-together barricade that would break once, maybe twice.

speaker: narrator
text: Villagers braced spears with white knuckles; Vale ran the line, tapping shoulders, fixing spacing, making fear into formation.

speaker: serana
text: "Reserve on the right,"

speaker: narrator
text: he told a sergeant.

speaker: serana
text: "When the paladin gives you the nod, you move. No heroics. We live."

speaker: narrator
text: The thralls came hard now, shadows flaring like oil under wind.

speaker: vex
text: "Thornik!"

speaker: narrator
text: Vex called from the parapet.

speaker: serana
text: "Left!"

speaker: narrator
text: He burned two charges to break a rush that would have folded the line. The belt went lighter at his ribs. He tapped the emptiness with a grim snort.

speaker: vex
text: "Three left. Then it's axes and prayer."

speaker: narrator
text: Nyxara was cool and surgical -- veils of suggestion and stutters of hesitation blooming in enemy minds, buying heartbeats where heartbeats meant survival. She did not reach for the deeper well yet. Not yet.

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin pushed harder. A spearline of red-gold leapt from his palm and cut a corridor through grasping shadows; a second cast flared broader, washing the cobbles clean for the bucket line to move. The lattice screamed inside his skin. He tasted copper. He did it anyway.

speaker: serana
text: "Hold!"

speaker: narrator
text: Serana called, catching a blow on the boss of her shield that would have broken a door. Her light flickered. She made it burn.

speaker: vex
text: "Hold -- !"

speaker: narrator
text: The line bent. It did not break.

speaker: narrator
text: Then three thralls slipped the ward seam Vex was guarding.

speaker: narrator
text: She cut one's knee, hamstrung the second, rolled under the third and took its throat clean -- then a fourth she hadn't seen came in low. The butt of its spear caught her temple. The world went sideways.

speaker: thornik
text: "Vex!"

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik shouted, trying to cut across, but the line surged to fill the gap and he was a beat too slow.

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin turned and saw her folded at an angle wrong for living things.

speaker: narrator
text: Something in him broke open.

speaker: narrator
text: He screamed.

speaker: narrator
text: Fire answered.

speaker: narrator
text: Not careful. Not neat. A blast-front roared across half the square, a dragon's exhale made narrow by brutal will. Thralls ignited in a red-gold bloom; their shadows screamed and fled like ink in boiling water. The nearest barrels scorched; bucket-men fell back with singed hair and wide eyes.

speaker: narrator
text: Pain tore him hollow. The lattice lit with searing white, every filament a wire pulled too taut. For a heartbeat he thought the scale would split his arm from the inside.

speaker: narrator
text: He stumbled through the heat he'd made, fell to his knees beside Vex.

speaker: narrator
text: Pulse -- where was her pulse --

speaker: narrator
text: There. Thready. There.

speaker: narrator
text: Footsteps. The old man dropped beside him, fingers already at Vex's throat, checking pupils with a thumb that forced her eyelid up with zero ceremony.

speaker: vex
text: "Concussion. Head trauma. Broken rib."

speaker: narrator
text: He started wrapping with brutal efficiency.

speaker: thornik
text: "Touch and go."

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin's throat closed.

speaker: vex
text: "Will she -- "

speaker: thornik
text: "Don't know yet."

speaker: narrator
text: No false comfort. Just bandages pulled tight.

speaker: vex
text: "Keep her breathing. Keep her still. That's all we can do right now."

speaker: narrator
text: The man looked up at Caelin's arm, at the scale visible under blood-stained bandages.

speaker: thornik
text: "Did you just scream fire across a battlefield?"

speaker: vex
text: "Yes."

speaker: thornik
text: "Objectively terrifying."

speaker: narrator
text: He tied off the wrap.

speaker: vex
text: "Also, thank you. She'd be dead without it."

speaker: narrator
text: He stood, moved to the next wounded, didn't look back.

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin stayed beside Vex, one hand near hers, counting breaths.

speaker: narrator
text: Movement -- wrong, close. A thrall's shadow reached toward his shoulder.

speaker: narrator
text: He had to move or die.

speaker: narrator
text: He stood through the pain and turned back into the fight.

speaker: narrator
text: The thralls had learned to avoid Serana and look for the thin places. A knot of them hit the well-head where the square narrowed. Vale went to meet them.

speaker: narrator
text: He fought like a man who'd practiced near a thousand nights for this one -- no wasted motion, no glory in it. He put his body where the line was thinnest and made it thick again. He looked toward Serana once; she nodded once; there was understanding.

speaker: narrator
text: When the spear came from the side -- slick black, more spike than wood -- he shoved a boy out of its path and took it clean through the ribs. He finished his stroke anyway, pinned one more shadow-thing to the stones, refused to fall until falling would close the gap he'd held.

speaker: thornik
text: "On me,"

speaker: narrator
text: he said once, voice wet, and the reserve swung like a gate because he'd taught them to hear that tone and obey it.

speaker: narrator
text: Serana saw him go. Her jaw set.

speaker: vex
text: "Hold,"

speaker: narrator
text: she said, softer, as if she could make the word a covering.

speaker: narrator
text: The old man reached him first, dropped beside him, hands already moving to assess the wound. Stopped. The spear had gone through cleanly, and what it carried in its wake was already spreading -- corruption, wrongness, the kind of wound no bandage could fix.

speaker: thornik
text: "I can fix broken,"

speaker: narrator
text: he said, voice tight with the particular frustration of a craftsman given an impossible job.

speaker: vex
text: "I can't fix this."

speaker: narrator
text: He met Vale's eyes -- still clear, still present -- and understood. Covered his face with clean cloth when it was done, stood, moved to the next patient who could be saved.

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik roared in Dwarvish and split a thrall at the collarbone. The shock of the blow traveled up his arms and lit old breaks; he kept swinging.

speaker: narrator
text: Durgan moved like a grim machine. Once, his shadow pulled toward the enemy's at the corners of light -- just a heartbeat, just enough to notice. He forced it flat and cut what needed cutting. Blood ran from his nose again; the collar caught it.

speaker: narrator
text: Nyxara finally reached down to the place she'd been avoiding.

speaker: narrator
text: She whispered in a language that wasn't spoken so much as remembered; frost formed along the lintels; breath smoked. Something stepped up out of absence at her call -- a hound built of rib-bones and blue witchlight, eyes like lanterns in fog.

speaker: thornik
text: "Fetch,"

speaker: narrator
text: she told it, voice steady. It went without hesitation and broke a thrall at the knees with a crack like a branch under ice.

speaker: narrator
text: A corpse behind the barricade rose at her second word -- its face wrapped in a strip of sackcloth she tied without flinching. It fought for them, clumsy, obedient, and bought four lives before it fell again.

speaker: narrator
text: The price walked into her hands. The gloves hid the color change, but she felt fingertips go colder, deader, one joint beginning to separate where tendon met bone. She bound that knowledge behind a perfect expression and kept working.

speaker: serana
text: "Now!"

speaker: narrator
text: Serana shouted, seeing the enemy weight shift to overcommitment. Her shield slammed into a leader's chest; the line stepped; the reserve flowed; the square held.

speaker: narrator
text: And then -- like a thread cut -- the thralls began to break.

speaker: narrator
text: They did not run so much as fold and vanish, remnants sinking back into cracks they had no right to fit.

speaker: narrator
text: Silence arrived the way it always does after a fight that large: not as absence but as presence, a new thing settling over the square with weight.

speaker: narrator
text: The moaning and dripping and the meaningless roll of a loose bucket across cobblestones -- all of it still there, all of it somehow part of the silence rather than breaking it.

speaker: narrator
text: The kind of quiet that means there is nothing left to kill and your body hasn't learned that yet.

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin's arm was a column of white heat from shoulder to wrist. He became aware, slowly, that he was still holding a fighting stance and made himself lower it. The lattice ticked and began to cool by degrees. He looked down at his hand. Still there.

speaker: narrator
text: Still attached. That felt like more than he deserved.

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik stood with an axe in each fist and his belt empty and blood on his beard that wasn't all his own. He ran his eyes across the square the way a man counts coin -- methodical, not hopeful. When he reached zero on the charge loops, he exhaled once through his nose. Then he put the axes away.

speaker: narrator
text: Durgan went very still at the square's edge. His shadow lay flat and honest at his boots in the torchlight. He pressed the back of his wrist to his nose. Dry. He checked again. Habit. He exhaled once, slow, like a man easing a door closed so it didn't wake anyone.

speaker: narrator
text: Vex hadn't moved. She was breathing. Caelin could see it from here. That was what mattered.

speaker: narrator
text: Silence after battle felt like a held breath finally released.

speaker: narrator
text: The third bell rang once, useless, telling a town that already knew to stop fighting because there was nothing left to kill.

speaker: narrator
text: Smoke crawled along the square's roofline like a cat learning to be brave. The smell was sweat, pitch, cooked iron, and the bitter, wrong scent the thralls left behind.

speaker: thornik
text: "Water on the east thatch!"

speaker: narrator
text: a sergeant called -- Vale's echo in his voice, trying to fill a space that couldn't be filled.

speaker: narrator
text: Aldric worked in the center of the square, surrounded by wounded, hands moving with the mechanical rhythm of someone past exhaustion who has no intention of stopping. When Caelin approached, he didn't look up from wrapping a farmer's arm.

speaker: serana
text: "Everyone needs a healer,"

speaker: narrator
text: he said.

speaker: thornik
text: "Get in line."

speaker: caelin
text: "She's still breathing,"

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin said quietly.

speaker: narrator
text: Aldric's hands stilled for just a heartbeat.

speaker: thornik
text: "Good. Keep it that way."

speaker: narrator
text: He tied off the bandage, moved to the next patient.

speaker: caelin
text: "Wake me if she stops."

speaker: narrator
text: They moved bodies to the south lane and covered them with clean sheets that smelled of sun and soap. Captain Vale lay among them, his hands crossed over the spear that had saved a boy who stood ten feet away and couldn't understand why he was still standing.

speaker: narrator
text: Serana knelt. Her light failed to gather. She prayed anyway, voice a rasp.

speaker: thornik
text: "Guide him. He kept his watch."

speaker: narrator
text: The words landed because she said them, light or no.

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik found the hinge at the east gate had splintered under blast and weight. No one asked. He set his hammer against the bent pin and began to work the metal back true. Tap, heat, wedge.

speaker: narrator
text: His tool-harness hung empty at his ribs, loops that should have held cleverness now just leather and air. He worked anyway, the old way, with sweat and stubbornness.

speaker: narrator
text: Nyxara did not linger in the square once the dead were counted and the living were moving. She took a narrow stair to the little room behind the archive, slid the bolt, and sat very still until the tremor in her hands stopped.

speaker: narrator
text: The gloves would come off later, in private. The accounting would be done where no one could see what she'd become.

speaker: narrator
text: Durgan cleaned his blades with method that could have been prayer. His shadow lay obedient at his boots; his shoulders were too square.

speaker: narrator
text: When the nosebleed started again, he pressed the heel of his hand to it and turned slightly so the dark cloth drank the red. He kept his eyes on Caelin across the square and told himself that standing here was still a choice he was making.

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin found Aldric on an upturned bucket at the square's edge, stitching a cut in his own forearm with the calm of a man mending a saddle.

speaker: caelin
text: "You do that yourself?"

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin asked.

speaker: thornik
text: "Every time."

speaker: narrator
text: Aldric bit the thread.

speaker: caelin
text: "Forty years of practice. You get good at it or you get dead, and I've always had a mild preference for the first option."

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin crouched.

speaker: thornik
text: "You're not from Millbrook."

speaker: caelin
text: "No."

speaker: narrator
text: Aldric rolled his sleeve down.

speaker: thornik
text: "Third Harrow Company, retired. Field surgeon fifteen years, then walking between towns for another ten patching whoever needed patching."

speaker: narrator
text: He paused.

speaker: caelin
text: "I was attached to the garrison at Veln's Post when the seals started failing two years ago. Forty men. Good men, most of them -- well-trained, sensible, the sort who ate their vegetables and didn't gamble more than they could lose."

speaker: narrator
text: He said it with the flat affect of someone who'd had time to turn the memory into something he could carry.

speaker: thornik
text: "Thirty-seven of them died to something that came through the lower vault. I couldn't cut it out. Couldn't bandage it. Couldn't do a single useful thing."

speaker: caelin
text: "You survived."

speaker: thornik
text: "I was in the east tower when it happened. A healer who was somewhere else when people needed healing."

speaker: narrator
text: He looked at his hands once, briefly, then didn't again.

speaker: caelin
text: "Since then I've been trying to get back into the plateau's border country. Find out what came through that vault. Find out if the other sealed vaults are next."

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin said, carefully:

speaker: thornik
text: "Veln's Post is Depthspire's eastern approach."

speaker: caelin
text: "I know where it is."

speaker: narrator
text: A beat.

speaker: thornik
text: "We're going in. North, toward Depthspire."

speaker: caelin
text: "I know what you are."

speaker: narrator
text: Aldric nodded at Caelin's wrapped arm.

speaker: thornik
text: "Saw the mark when you were on your knees beside the woman. Knew what you were about."

speaker: narrator
text: He stood, joints filing their usual complaints.

speaker: caelin
text: "You'll need a healer. That plateau has spent the last two years demonstrating that it can open people up in ways that don't respond well to wishful thinking."

speaker: vex
text: "Sounds like a reason to stay away,"

speaker: narrator
text: Vex said from behind Caelin. He hadn't heard her cross the square.

speaker: aldric
text: "That's what the thirty-seven thought,"

speaker: narrator
text: Aldric said pleasantly,

speaker: vex
text: "before they stopped having opinions."

speaker: narrator
text: He looked at Vex's shoulder with the eyes of a man who'd assessed a great many wounds and was unimpressed by what he was seeing.

speaker: aldric
text: "That wrap needs redoing. Sit down."

speaker: narrator
text: Vex sat down.

speaker: narrator
text: She woke near midnight. Caelin was there, which she registered before she registered anything else.

speaker: vex
text: "Six,"

speaker: narrator
text: he said, when her eyes focused.

speaker: aldric
text: "Before you fell. In case you were counting."

speaker: vex
text: "Seven,"

speaker: narrator
text: she said.

speaker: aldric
text: "You missed one at the drainpipe."

speaker: narrator
text: He almost smiled.

speaker: vex
text: "How's your head?"

speaker: aldric
text: "Feels like someone rang it like a bell."

speaker: narrator
text: She shifted, winced, settled.

speaker: vex
text: "Vale?"

speaker: narrator
text: The almost-smile went away.

speaker: aldric
text: "He saved people. We'll say his name right."

speaker: narrator
text: She was quiet a moment. Then:

speaker: vex
text: "This Aldric. He any good?"

speaker: aldric
text: "He stitched his own arm without flinching and told a man with a broken leg that it was a net positive. I think he'll do."

speaker: vex
text: "Mm."

speaker: narrator
text: She closed her eyes.

speaker: aldric
text: "Don't let him touch my shoulder again while I'm awake. He's not gentle."

speaker: vex
text: "He said you'd say that. He said if you say that, he was right about the wrap and you can thank him later."

speaker: narrator
text: Vex said something short and accurate about Aldric's bedside manner and went back to sleep.

speaker: narrator
text: Night came early, as it does after battles. Millbrook slept in ragged shifts.

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin sat outside on the stable step and watched the sky find stars one at a time.

speaker: narrator
text: The scale had settled lower than it had been in days -- the scream-cast had burned something down to a truer level, cleared some pressure he hadn't known had been building. He flexed his fingers in the dark.

speaker: narrator
text: The lattice answered steady and sullen, like a fire that had stopped trying to be impressive and committed to just burning.

speaker: narrator
text: He looked north. The scale warmed a fraction -- the same direction it had been pointing since the mountain, patient as a compass needle. Somewhere out there the Depthspire aurora had written curtains across the sky.

speaker: narrator
text: Somewhere out there L's note said two weeks. Somewhere out there thirty-seven men from Veln's Post were owed an answer.

speaker: narrator
text: The third bell had rung too late. Millbrook held, but Vale was gone, and the cost of holding felt heavier than the victory.

speaker: narrator
text: He let that sit. He wasn't going to argue with it. He let it settle beside the other things he was carrying, and then he went in to sleep in pieces, the way you do after battles, and let the north wait until morning.
